briefing.headline: Rig up a 10" Worm — aggressive summer approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The 10" Worm is a strong choice for summer with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: An old-school TX-rigged 6-inch worm on ledges is something people forget about — you can go behind other anglers and get bites when fish won't eat a crankbait or are not grouped up. Use 3/8-oz tungsten, 4/0 hook, 14-lb fluorocarbon

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: An old-school TX-rigged 6-inch worm on ledges is something people forget about — you can go behind other anglers and get bites when fish won't eat a crankbait or are not grouped up. Use 3/8-o
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on ledge, grass, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When post-spawn schools bust up due to high water, Texas-rig a big worm (Strike King Bullworm) in green pumpkin to find small scattered schools.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on ledge, grass, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'I like to fish offshore and target shell bars or brushpiles with a 10-inch worm in summer.'
Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'
Johnston: I need the sun and the calm. Most people want the wind to blow, but I want it to be really tough on everybody because I figured out a method to catch them when it's really slick.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal summer bass feed aggressively. Fish split between shallow morning patterns (2-6ft) and offshore structure (12-25ft). Target grass lines, points, and ledges as falling pressure triggers movement.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
